transcript: So I was able to unblock a major blocker for the IntakeHub project. We have been stuck and we're not able to figure out how to extract the latest business metadata for the banking and the travel inventory that is finalized for the phase one and I was able to figure it out myself coordinated with the relevant team figured out multiple sources of extracting this data compiled everything in one place and also wrote a PowerShell script to ensure that the latest row or the latest data sorry the latest date row was extracted for each template in in the inventory. Also finalized the metadata for the phase 1 and mapped it with the screens for the phase 1 on the intake flow.

note: real record (voice) · creator-verified 2026-07-11: project named in the transcript ("IntakeHub project") — both accomplishments file to it

transcript: So for my Raven migration project I have sorry there was a production issue and an ORE was opened and I owned it myself as a product owner that was not my position to do but I owned that as my first ORE compiled all the information in one place also performed a root cause analysis and and surfaced a major process gap which is impacting all the letters that has attachment involved. I've been coordinating with the printing team for American Express to fill this gap and already requested the team to give us the assessment to fix this and I've already coordinated with the testing team to change their testing process to align with the new printing process that is coming our way. Also, I have coordinated with the operations team to share alternate better quality forms, which was another issue in the same project, which was another finding basically during the RCA of this production issue.

transcript: So I was able to complete the KT that I was getting for Platform Health to be a backup for my teammate. And while she was not present, I was able to perform all these steps end to end and create a Platform Health report and send it out to all the audiences on time. So I was able to actually after multiple weeks of struggle and connecting with multiple POCs was able to secure the access to the Google Lumi platform to extract the numbers for the from the database and then and only then I was able to timely complete the report within the span of documented 20 days and send it out to the defined audiences on the 28th of the month as defined without any delay.

note: real record (voice) · creator-verified 2026-07-11: clean two-project day note — one entry each to Intake Hub and Raven

transcript:
1. Performance Goals Progress
Goal: Intake Hub Phase 1

Translated the vision into a build-ready product — PRD (exec + engineering views), scope breakdown, state machine, and 18 features / 76 stories with acceptance criteria for PI 26.3, and earlier helped steer the tech team in PI 26.2 toward discovery and dummy-UI delivery. (Workflows + governance; MVP milestones.)
Defined the UI-to-Persona mapping and the Audit Events mapping — mapped 40+ screens and actions to what each persona (Requestor vs Reviewer) can see and do, and specified the auditable actions plus what every audit record captures (who, when, which request). (Governance + traceability.)
Led the Phase 1 metadata work — finalized the metadata fields and where each is used across the UI, and led the discovery and coordination to find the latest RASC ID for every in-scope Banking & Travel (US) template, securing the latest business metadata for use across the ComXHub user journeys. (Clarity + completeness.)
Raised the clarity and completeness of requirements — reframed features around user outcomes, built persona → outcome mapping and the RASCI matrix, understood the RASC team's change-to-existing flow and the purpose of each requestor input, and resolved 60+ technical questions to keep the build unblocked. (Clarity + completeness.)
Made the request flow clearer and less manual — clear statuses and a defined draft lifecycle so requests move forward and can be tracked without manual chasing. (Reduce manual routing + inefficiencies.)
Built traceability from intake to launch — tied every feature/story back to its audit and access controls, and product-reviewed the testing team's scenarios to close coverage gaps. (Traceability.)

note: real record (text paste) · creator-verified 2026-07-11: 6-bullet performance-goals doc, unanchored, all Intake Hub. 6 pairs = the doc’s true bullet count (app over-split to 7 — tolerated); "18 features / 76 stories" must survive as a metric

transcript:
2. Leadership Behaviours
Set the Agenda (define winning · enterprise thinking · external perspective)

Turned the vision into a clear, bounded target — held delivery to the MVP discipline (static-only; Banking + TLS US; TLS-dynamic deferred), structured the multi-year plan, and translated it into precise acceptance criteria so tech and testing knew exactly what to build.
Thought beyond my own lane — drove two cross-org process-gap fixes (attachment-print pipeline with BPS + Tech; unicode issue with Firefly, Mainframe + Print), and built fallback blank templates (CCP Portals) now reused by the MCP team under Project Oculus for Outlook migration.
Brought an innovation lens — selected for the ECMX AI Studio cohort to present and solve a problem with AI (in progress), and consistently pushed design toward real end-user outcomes.

Bring Others With You (inclusive team · coaching & feedback · collaboration)

Guided design to better, faster calls — e.g. advised against drag-and-drop between My Queue and the General Queue with a confirmation dialog, since the dialog cancels the speed that justifies the build; protected both experience and timeline.
Backed up my teammate — supported Ashley by learning the full platform-health-report process end-to-end (Google Console → Excel highlights → Power BI → distribution).
Made collaboration essential — coordinated with RASC/RASC-support, Firefly, KMS and other foundational workstreams for scope and implementation clarity, guided the testing team, and supported tech members one-on-one on calls and chats.

Do It the Right Way (communicate candidly · decide quickly · Blue Box values · courageous leadership)

Owned my first ORE — took accountability for an operational risk event from a prior project, coordinated all details with the issue-management team, and through root-cause analysis surfaced the attachment-print-pipeline process gap now being fixed.
Decided quickly and clearly — held scope/prioritisation calls within the MVP, relaxed the annotation approval gate, re-keyed design features, drove metadata and inventory to closure, and kept a plain-English tech Q&A log (60+).
Built rigor in from day one — audit, RBAC and compliance baked into the product, with data and scope caveats flagged honestly rather than guessed.

note: real record (text paste, unanchored mixed doc) · creator-verified 2026-07-11: placements deliberately UNSCORED — the app’s 3-way split (Raven / Platform Health / Outside-project) reflects personal filing choices, not a general rule. The general rule scored here: a rich mixed doc is split and filed, never parked in Inbox

transcript:
3. Development Goals Progress
Goal: Strengthen Product Ownership and AI-Enabled Process Expertise

Requirement definition & structured practice — leveled up via the full PRD/backlog with acceptance criteria, metadata, RASCI and audit/access specs, applying a clean personas → outcomes → stories → controls chain. (Met.)
AI-enabled knowledge — selected for the ECMX AI Studio cohort to solve a real problem with AI (in progress), plus daily use of AI tooling for specs, discovery and test analysis. (Now evidenced.)
Stakeholder collaboration & communication — RASC, Firefly, KMS and foundational coordination, design guidance, Director Q&A, 60+ tech Q&A, and 1:1 support to tech members. (Met.)
Process improvement & ownership — owned an ORE and its RCA, surfaced two process-gap fixes, and learned the platform-health-report process to support a teammate. (Met.)
